what_it_does: Implements a self-contained, mountable brand-icon component: an SVG Vi 'V' mark with concentric spinner rings, state-linked color and animation, left/double/right-click handlers, and an exported CSS string.
observations: Single SVG with viewBox 0 0 512 512 holds both the logo paths and the rings centered at 256,256.
Logo fill and ring stroke both use currentColor so one color assignment synchronizes them.
Imports resolveStateColor from ./palette.js but also keeps a local offline fallback color map.
Supported states are idle, launching, thinking, tool-use, error, and success.
Distinguishes single click from double click using a 250 ms timeout.
Right-click is intercepted for a radial wheel and native context menu is suppressed.
Exports BRAND_ICON_CSS as a string for shadow-DOM or style injection.
Returns an API with setState, setFocusActive, getBounds, and getters for state, color, and element.
standards: The click/double-click timeout adds a noticeable 250 ms delay to every single click.
CSS is bundled as a string export rather than a separate stylesheet, making the component portable.
conventions: Component carries its own styles (starter pattern).
Prefers imported palette source with a local fallback.
JSDoc annotates the public factory API.
concerns: The 250 ms single/double-click delay can make the UI feel sluggish and may mishandle rapid clicks.
If ./palette.js fails to load, the import throws before the offline fallback can be used.
stateColors duplicates palette data already maintained elsewhere.
console.log in setState may be noisy in production.
There is no destroy/dispose method to remove listeners or clear the pending click timeout.
getBounds returns a live DOMRect but does not transform coordinates for the caller.
notes: A portable, state-animated brand icon that packages its own SVG geometry, interaction surface, and stylesheet.
questions: Is the 250 ms click delay acceptable for the target interaction model?
How is BRAND_ICON_CSS actually injected in production (shadow DOM style path is mentioned but not implemented here)?
What should happen if resolveStateColor returns a color shape different from the offline map?
Should the component expose a teardown method?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to reconcile a legacy component API with recovered authentic geometry while adding state-linked visual feedback; the file does implement that API and geometry, plus self-contained CSS.
intention_signals: It is trying to make the brand icon reusable outside overlay.js by bundling SVG, behavior, and styles together, while keeping the exact API and visual identity that existing callers expect.
novelty: Bundles recovered 3-path V geometry, animated spinner rings, and a full CSS animation stylesheet into one exported component module.
contribution: Provides a mountable brand icon with state-driven color and ring animations, a click/double-click/right-click interaction surface, and a portable CSS string, so it can be embedded in different hosts without relying on overlay.js styles.
summary_for_embedding: This file exports a factory function that builds a stateful Vi brand icon (SVG V mark plus spinner rings) with click/double-click/right-click handlers, color and animation state transitions, and a self-contained CSS string.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to preserve an existing brand-icon API while replacing the internals with recovered authentic geometry and making the component portable via bundled styles.

symbols.does: Builds the single SVG element containing the V logo paths and concentric spinner rings.
symbols.description: Creates an SVG with viewBox 0 0 512 512, groups the spinner rings behind the logo, and uses currentColor for both ring strokes and logo fills so a single color assignment drives the whole icon.

symbols.does: Factory that mounts the brand icon into a container and returns the public API.
symbols.description: Creates the DOM element, appends the SVG, wires left-click/double-click and right-click handlers, exposes setState, setFocusActive, getBounds, and initializes the icon to the idle state.

symbols.does: Updates the icon's logical state, color, and ring animation classes.
symbols.description: Resolves the state's color from the palette or fallback map, applies it via currentColor and a CSS custom property, toggles focus-active and ring spin classes based on whether the state is active, and logs the transition.

symbols.does: Temporarily boosts visual focus (glow + ring spin) without changing the logical state.
symbols.description: Adds focus styling and ring animations when active; only removes them if the current logical state is idle or success, so processing states keep the rings spinning.

symbols.does: Returns the icon element's bounding client rectangle.
symbols.description: Provides a geometry accessor used by callers such as radial-wheel positioning to locate the icon on screen.
